title: aster foliaceus subsp. apricus
intro: Aster foliaceus subsp. apricus, also known as the apricot-leaved aster, is a subspecies of the aster plant that is characterized by its broad, apricot-colored leaves and small white or pink flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and combat infections. The health benefits of Aster foliaceus subsp. apricus include its ability to alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and bronchitis, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
